title: Macrosolen pallens
taxonomy: Macrosolen pallens is classified within the family Loranthaceae. This plant is also known as a species of mistletoe. The family Loranthaceae is a group of parasitic flowering plants. Macrosolen pallens, along with the rest of the Loranthaceae family, is native to tropical regions of Asia. These plants are known to obtain nutrients by attaching themselves to the branches of their host trees.

intro: Macrosolen pallens, also known as the "Indian periploca," is a plant species in the Apocynaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include treating various health conditions such as fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Macrosolen pallens include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Traditional herbal preparations made with Macrosolen pallens include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, particularly respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
